topic.what: Training ML models on encrypted data using CKKS homomorphic encryption scheme
topic.how: Implements CKKS-based homomorphic operations enabling KNN, linear regression, and MLP inference on encrypted data via polynomial arithmetic
topic.why_matters: Enables end-to-end privacy preservation where sensitive data remains encrypted throughout ML training pipeline

characteristics.what_it_measures: Model prediction quality via R-squared on Housing dataset
characteristics.evidence: Table 2: Linear Regression R2=0.70, KNN R2=0.75, MLP R2=0.70
characteristics.context: Encrypted models achieve near-identical accuracy to plaintext equivalents on Boston Housing dataset

characteristics.what_it_measures: Root mean squared error for regression models
characteristics.evidence: Table 1-2: Encrypted KNN RMSE=4.79, Linear Regression RMSE=5.14, MLP RMSE=5.15
characteristics.context: Error magnitudes acceptable for real-world regression tasks

characteristics.evidence: Section 3.1: Testing confirmed errors below 10^-6 for inputs normalized to [-1,1]
characteristics.context: CKKS approximate arithmetic introduces minimal numerical error

characteristics.what_itasures: Time required to fit models on encrypted data
characteristics.evidence: Table 3: Encrypted Linear Regression fit=0.35s, KNN fit=0.08s
characteristics.context: Proof-of-concept with reduced parameters; real-world would be significantly slower

characteristics.evidence: Table 3: Linear Regression predict=0.10s, KNN predict=1.55s on encrypted data
characteristics.context: KNN especially costly due to distance computation to all training points

characteristics.what_it_measures: Encryption-induced performance penalty relative to plaintext
characteristics.evidence: Table 3 shows Linear Regression fit overhead ~1.6x, KNN training ~188x
characteristics.context: Overhead depends on operation count, noise management, polynomial degree

novelty: demonstrates feasibility of training KNN and linear regression entirely on encrypted data
provides custom CKKS implementation for ML operations
shows encrypted inference for MLP with linear activations
validates accuracy preservation across encrypted/plaintext model equivalence
open_problems: efficient non-polynomial activation approximations under encryption
scaling to larger datasets and real-world parameter settings
eliminating trusted third-party dependency via threshold or multi-key HE
managing noise accumulation in deep computation pipelines
